mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives positive equity returns exclusively during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the predictive coefficient flips negative in that regime.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal expanding economic activity and wage growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC below 0.01 across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 210.0, elevated inflation expectations trigger anticipated monetary tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 210.0 are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly declines in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirm a broad-based demand contraction, which forces corporate earnings downgrades and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Co-occurring declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates below 0.5 percent sustain equity valuations through compressed discount rates, but this positive return mechanism only activates during liquidity-abundant regimes.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS below 0.5 percent fails to predict positive 5-day returns during liquidity-abundant periods, or the signal reverses direction when liquidity conditions tighten.
